observations.label: visible_elements
observations.value: narrow interior corridor, exposed rough stone or masonry wall on the right, painted plaster wall on the left, light-colored floor tiles in the corridor, wood or laminate flooring in the foreground, small step/threshold transition between floor finishes, small sink and countertop/cabinet at the far end, recessed ceiling light, baseboards/trim along walls, loose cables on the floor in the foreground, trash bin and broom at left foreground

observations.label: unclear_regions
observations.value: foreground floor transition detail and exact height of step, condition behind/under the sink cabinet, whether the exposed wall is intentionally unfinished or stripped for repair, presence of moisture, efflorescence, or active water damage on the exposed wall, full ceiling condition near the right upper edge
observations.notes: Regions the model could not inspect confidently.

observations.label: space_type
observations.value: narrow interior passage or kitchenette corridor
observations.notes: Small sink and cabinet visible at the end of the corridor.

observations.label: right_wall_finish
observations.value: exposed masonry/stone with irregular plaster remnants
observations.notes: Large portion of right wall appears stripped back to rough substrate.

observations.label: left_wall_finish
observations.notes: Left wall appears finished and painted beige.

observations.label: floor_finish_corridor
observations.notes: Light-colored square tiles visible in the corridor.

observations.label: floor_finish_foreground
observations.notes: Different flooring material visible before the step.

observations.label: level_change
observations.notes: A step or raised threshold separates foreground floor from tiled corridor.

observations.label: trip_hazard
observations.notes: Step transition and loose cables in foreground increase access/safety risk.

observations.label: sink_present
observations.notes: Small sink with faucet and undercounter cabinet is visible at the end.

observations.label: ceiling_light_present
observations.notes: One recessed ceiling light is on.

observations.label: site_access_constraint
observations.notes: Tight corridor may limit material handling and labor productivity.

observations.label: housekeeping_clutter
observations.notes: Trash bin, broom, bottles, and cables are present but passage remains mostly open.

clarifying_questions.question: Is the exposed right wall intended to remain visible stone/masonry, or is it to be replastered/finished?
clarifying_questions.reason: Finish scope changes labor, materials, and preparation substantially.

clarifying_questions.question: Is there any active dampness, water ingress, or salt/efflorescence on the exposed wall?
clarifying_questions.reason: Moisture problems would require diagnosis and remedial treatment before finishing.

clarifying_questions.question: What is the exact height and required treatment of the floor level change at the corridor entrance?
clarifying_questions.reason: Threshold/transition details affect flooring, trim, and safety pricing.

clarifying_questions.question: Will the sink/cabinet area remain in place, be replaced, or need protection during works?
clarifying_questions.reason: Affects demolition/protection scope and access in the narrow corridor.

clarifying_questions.question: Are the loose cables in the foreground temporary, or do they require permanent rerouting/finishing?
clarifying_questions.reason: Could add electrical or carpentry scope and affects site safety.

visible_elements: exposed rough stone or masonry wall on the right
painted plaster wall on the left
light-colored floor tiles in the corridor
wood or laminate flooring in the foreground
small step/threshold transition between floor finishes
small sink and countertop/cabinet at the far end

trash bin and broom at left foreground
unclear_regions: foreground floor transition detail and exact height of step
whether the exposed wall is intentionally unfinished or stripped for repair
presence of moisture, efflorescence, or active water damage on the exposed wall
full ceiling condition near the right upper edge
